name: Return of the Mac
intro: This mac ‘n’ cheese is creamy, cheesy, savory and sharp. It will taste as if you’re eating the dairy version. It is one of my best sellers at my pop-up in New York City. We’re going to use potatoes, carrots and cauliflower for sharpness. Nutritional yeast will be added for more of a cheesy flavor. This recipe requires patience and love.
method: In a large pot, bring 4 cups (946 ml) of water to a boil and add 1 tablespoon (18 g) of salt, the potatoes, carrot and cauliflower and boil for 10 minutes, or until tender. Drain the vegetables but reserve 2 cups (480 ml) of the water. Add the vegetables to a blender with the nutritional yeast, melted vegan butter, oregano, paprika, garlic powder, hot sauce and pepper. Then blend, adding the reserved 2 cups (480 ml) of water gradually until you create a creamy and loose sauce.

Preheat the oven to 400°F (200°C). In a large pot (rinse and reuse the one used for the vegetables), bring 6 cups (1.4 L) of water and 1½ teaspoons (9 g) of salt to a boil. Once the water is boiling, add the macaroni and boil until al dente.

Drain the macaroni in a strainer and place it in a 9 x 13–inch (23 x 33–cm) baking dish, followed by the vegetable mixture, and mix everything together. Add a layer of vegan Cheddar cheese shreds on top and cover with foil. Bake for 15 minutes, then remove the foil and broil the cheese for 6 minutes, or until it browns up a bit.
